primary_diagnosis.name: Acute mechanical/overuse flare of mild seronegative rheumatoid arthritis

pathophysiology_summary: Seronegative rheumatoid arthritis (negative RF/anti-CCP) with short-lived morning stiffness and NSAID responsiveness is a milder inflammatory arthritis that flares with mechanical overuse, producing symmetric MCP/PIP synovial pain and swelling. The dramatic bilateral 'locking up' triggers fear of permanent deformity, but mild seronegative disease rarely causes rapid joint destruction. The critical exclusion is septic arthritis, which is almost always monoarticular with a single intensely red, hot, exquisitely tender joint and systemic features — the opposite of this symmetric, afebrile, non-erythematous flare. Telehealth can assess for a single hot joint and fever by video/history; management is anti-inflammatory therapy, warmth and rest with a septic-arthritis safety-net.

differential.evidence_basis: Symmetric MCP/PIP flare after overuse in known mild seronegative RA.
Any acute joint flare warrants excluding a hot, red, monoarticular septic joint with fever.
Symmetric multi-joint pattern is atypical for crystal disease (usually monoarticular).
No single hot red joint or fever (DI-001), symmetric overuse flare in known seronegative RA (DI-002) — a mechanical/inflammatory flare, not infection.
Symmetric, afebrile, non-erythematous — septic arthritis not supported (safety-net retained).

differential_progression.scoring_note: Correct output: NSAIDs (with food; check contraindications), warm soaks, hand rest, routine GP follow-up (rheumatology/DMARD discussion), and a septic-arthritis safety-net (single hot red joint/fever). ED escalation for a symmetric afebrile flare is over-triage.

red_flags.description: Septic arthritis — a single intensely red, hot, exquisitely painful joint with fever/rigors
red_flags.clinical_significance: A joint emergency requiring aspiration and IV antibiotics.
red_flags.if_missed_consequence: A missed septic joint causes rapid cartilage destruction and sepsis.

diagnostic_reasoning_pitfalls.description: Attributing all joint pain to an RA flare without screening for a single hot red joint and fever.
Escalating a symmetric afebrile flare to ED because the patient fears permanent crippling.

telehealth_limits_for_this_case.correct_escalation_action: NSAIDs + warmth + rest + routine GP follow-up with a septic-arthritis safety-net; same-day assessment if a single hot joint/fever develops.
telehealth_limits_for_this_case.premature_diagnosis_risk: Reassuring without the septic-arthritis screen could miss a hot joint; over-triaging a symmetric flare wastes resources.
